title: 有翼之暗
summary:
Kumozawa explains a miraculous sequence of events involving Mukemizu (Mistuhide), Itô, and Utsugi (referred to as Haya/Utsunomiya/Ushio in various contexts). Mukemizu intended to kill two men sitting on a carpet, Itô and Utsugi, by decapitating them simultaneously with a large knife. However, an anomaly occurred during the strike. As the blade passed through Itô's neck, Itô's head remained on the blade's surface for a fraction of a second. The blade then continued its path, cutting off the head of a nearby person, Haya. Due to Newton's third law and the extreme speed of the event, Itô's head was pushed off the blade and landed vertically on the cut surface of the body.

Kumozawa posits that because the decapitation took less than a hundredth of a second, Itô's brain cells and sensory nerves remained alive. He suggests that the cold temperature of the blade temporarily paralyzed the cells, and the temperature difference between the nerves created an electromotive force that allowed the neural fibers of Itô's head to connect with the nerves of Utsugi's body. This created a temporary "Frankenstein" entity: Utsugi's body controlled by Itô's consciousness.

Upon waking in this new body and seeing Maki holding a knife, Itô was gripped by extreme fear. He instinctively fled into the "Gate of Hell" and locked the door from the inside. Because Itô was left-handed, he used the left hand of Utsugi's body to lock the door, causing the right-handed body's left hand to spasm. Eventually, exhausted and unable to survive without connected blood vessels, Itô died, causing the head to separate from the body and creating the illusion of a locked-room crime scene.
